title: Antrophyum hookerianum
taxonomy: Antrophyum hookerianum is classified under the plant family Arecaceae and is a member of the genus Antrophyum. The genus Antrophyum belongs to the order Arecales and the class Liliopsida. This classification places Antrophyum hookerianum within the monocots, a group of flowering plants. It is also known as the Hooker's Antrophyum fern, which might indicate its classification in the broader plant kingdom. This classification is based on various botanical sources and is subject to change as new research emerges.

intro: Antrophyum hookerianum, also known as the Hooker's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Antrophyum hookerianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the Hooker's maidenhair fern is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory infections.
